diagnosis.summary: The acceptance request failed in the resumable Agent integration around the deterministic Office gate, but the available production access logs cannot attribute individual CodeAPI /exec calls to this conversation. Post-rollback isolation probes proved that createBashExecutionTool().invoke(), the real codeEnvRef, file injection, and /mnt/data visibility all work in 54-60 ms. The remaining fault boundary is the real web-request selection/lifecycle path, including abort persistence, rather than Excel parsing or CodeAPI availability.
evidence: The current-turn file upload reached CodeAPI at 2026-07-19T14:22:47Z.
The stored user message contains a complete codeEnvRef and updateFilesUsage returns complete Mongo file documents.
primeFiles returns id, storage_session_id, name, and kind values compatible with the gate's intended matching contract.
Post-rollback direct transport probes returned in 54-60 ms and mounted acceptance.xlsx under /mnt/data.
The running package resolves @librechat/api to /app/packages/api/dist/index.cjs, so the deployment target path was correct.
Mongo persisted an empty assistant row 213.861 seconds after the user message with unfinished=false and error=false.
The direct pre-parse invoke had no bounded timeout and was not wired to the request AbortSignal.
The pre-parse unit test stubbed the tool invoke and did not cover the real ResumableAgentController, live file normalization, CodeAPI ToolMessage response, or user-stop persistence path.
The existing semantic-empty guard runs after model completion and does not protect every resumable initialization or abort persistence path.

decision: Do not redeploy this artifact. Before any replacement implementation, add controller-level coverage for real requestFiles normalization, current-turn file selection, real ToolMessage parsing, bounded abort-aware execution, and a user Stop before visible assistant content.
